description_long: This object consists of two distinct mushrooms standing side by side. The larger mushroom has a broad, dome-shaped cap with a matte brown surface and a thick, cylindrical stem that is darker at the base and lighter at the top. The smaller mushroom features a vibrant red cap adorned with numerous small white spots, giving it a classic Amanita muscaria appearance, and a similarly shaped but slightly thinner stem. Both mushrooms have smooth, unbroken caps with no visible gills from any angle. The stems are robust and appear to be solid, supporting the caps above ground level. The overall texture of the mushrooms is matte and slightly rough, with the red mushroom's cap being more visually striking due to its color and pattern.
description: A pair of mushrooms: one large with a matte brown cap and one smaller with a red, white-spotted cap, both with thick stems.
description_view_0: Front view showing both mushrooms side by side, highlighting the brown cap on the left and the red, spotted cap on the right.
description_view_1: Rear view with the brown mushroom mostly obscuring the red mushroom, only the red cap's edge is visible.
description_view_2: Side view with the red, spotted mushroom on the left and the brown mushroom on the right, both caps and stems clearly visible.
description_view_3: Angled view focusing on the brown mushroom, with the red mushroom mostly hidden behind.
attribute_cap_pattern: brown smooth, red with white spots
